clusters.title: World Cup quick hits: Salah's Socceroos boost and the tightest call
clusters.neutral_headline: World Cup quick hits: Salah's Socceroos boost and the tightest call
bullet_summary: Reported by ABC Just In, ABC Sport

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Salah's Socceroos boost and the tightest call
items.standfirst: The Socceroos' next World Cup opponents Egypt are sweating on the fitness of key striker Mohamed Salah, Scotland's coach Steve Clarke resigns after his team's exit and Davinson Sánchez was denied a late winner because of his toe. Here are today's quick hits.
items.content: The Socceroos' next World Cup opponents Egypt are sweating on the fitness of key striker Mohamed Salah, Scotland's coach Steve Clarke resigns after his team's exit and Davinson Sánchez was denied a late winner because of his toe. Here are today's quick hits.

items.title: History beckons but a 'fearless' opponent awaits the Socceroos
items.standfirst: Egypt is sweating on the fitness of star forward Mohamed Salah, but the Socceroos won't be complacent as they prepare for just the third FIFA World Cup knockout encounter in the team's history.
items.content: Egypt is sweating on the fitness of star forward Mohamed Salah, but the Socceroos won't be complacent as they prepare for just the third FIFA World Cup knockout encounter in the team's history.
